rulings.Case Title: CARA E. VAINISH VS. MATTHEW L. KLEIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Vainish V Klein Plaintiff Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 29, 2020 line 1. PLAINTIFF CARA VAINISH Vainish V Klein Plaintiff Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgement Hearing Required to address the enforceability of the account splitting provision. Settlement estimated an "approximate amount of $20,000." Per Plaintiff, the amount is closer to $9,000. (The Court notes that approximately $18,000 originally tendered would be in line with the provisions of the settlement agreement). This provision of the settlement agreement appears to have been reached based on unilateral or mutual mistake. Parties to address is this provision is still enforceable, if it is severable from the rest of the agreement, and can the judgement be entered if this part of the agreement is vacated for parties to proceed on this issue to trial. Motion is GRANTED as to the issue regarding $500 payments associated with the tenant's occupancy from July 2019 to December 2019. Depending on the outcome on the first issue, Plaintiff may obtain a judgement for $3,000, subject to any payments already made for those months from the escrow account. Both sides to prepare proposed orders reflecting their respective positions on this motion. Proposed orders to be submitted to Dept501Orders@sftc.org no later than 9:00 a.m. on October 29, 2020. =(501/CFH)
